title: Universal Spawn Manifest
description: Canonical JSON Schema for a universal-spawn manifest v1.0.0. A single declarative file that makes any creation discoverable, validatable, and one-click spawnable across AI, hosting, creative, dev, game, social, and hardware platforms.

spawn_version.description: Version of the universal-spawn spec this manifest targets. Use semantic versioning. Consumers MUST reject manifests with an unknown major version.

id.description: Globally unique reverse-DNS identifier for the creation. Lowercase, dot-separated, no spaces. Used as the stable key across registries.

name.description: Human-readable display name. Shown in UIs. Must not contain emoji; enforce via lint since JSON Schema regex support for Unicode property escapes is uneven across validators.

kind.description: The primary category of this creation. Determines which platforms can spawn it and what fields are expected.

description.description: One-paragraph description. Observational, not marketing. No emoji. First sentence MUST stand alone as a summary (used in search snippets).

summary.description: Optional shorter one-line summary (<=140 chars) for cards and social embeds.

icon.description: Relative path or URL to a 512x512 icon. SVG preferred. Follows the Residual Frequencies visual conventions when applicable.

hero_plate.description: Relative path or URL to a hero plate illustration (see design/residual-frequencies.md for archetypes). Used on rich cards and README top.

license.description: SPDX license identifier, or 'proprietary'. Manifests under the universal-spawn standard SHOULD use OSI-approved licenses.

author.description: Primary author / maintainer of the creation.

source.description: Where the canonical source of this creation lives. The repository MUST contain this manifest at its root.

commit.description: Optional exact commit hash pinning the manifest snapshot.

path.description: Subdirectory inside the repository, if the creation is a monorepo child.

keywords.description: Lowercase keywords for discovery. Max 16. Each <= 32 chars.

categories.description: High-level categories. Max 4. Values SHOULD come from the canonical taxonomy (see spec/v1.0.0/spec.md Appendix A).

runtime.description: Runtime requirements. What does this creation need to actually run?

dependencies.description: Other spawn manifests this creation depends on.

entrypoints.description: Declarative entrypoints. Each entrypoint is an action a spawning platform can invoke. No executable code is ever inlined — only declarative references.

env_vars_required.description: Environment variables the creation requires. NEVER hardcode secret values here — only declare that they are required and describe their purpose.

min_permissions.description: The smallest set of capabilities this creation needs to function. Spawn platforms MUST NOT grant capabilities outside this set. See spec Appendix B for canonical permission strings. A permission is `namespace:action` or `namespace:action:scope`; namespace and action use kebab-case lowercase letters and digits; scope accepts hostnames, paths, and key=value pairs.

rate_limit_qps.description: Self-declared maximum outbound request rate the creation will produce, in queries per second.

cost_limit_usd_daily.description: Self-declared upper bound on daily USD cost this creation can incur when spawned with default config. Spawn platforms SHOULD enforce this as a hard ceiling.

safe_for_auto_spawn.description: True IFF the author asserts this manifest is safe to spawn without human confirmation under min_permissions and the declared cost/rate limits. Platforms MAY still require confirmation.

data_residency.description: Regions where data generated by this creation may legally reside.

platforms.description: Platform-specific extensions. Each key is a platform id (see platforms/). Each value is the platform's extension object, validated against that platform's schema.extension.json.

spawn_targets.description: Ordered list of platforms this creation prefers to be spawned on. First entry is the canonical target.

grok_install.description: Compatibility with AgentMindCloud/grok-install. Set version to the minimum grok-install version that can consume this manifest.

mapping_file.description: Optional relative path to a mapping file that translates this manifest into a grok-install manifest.

openapi.description: Relative path to an OpenAPI document, if applicable.

dockerfile.description: Relative path to a Dockerfile, if applicable.

signatures.description: Detached signatures over the canonical serialization of this manifest. See spec Appendix C for the canonicalization procedure.

x-ext.description: Free-form escape hatch for experimental fields. Keys MUST begin with a reverse-DNS vendor prefix. Consumers MAY ignore all x-ext content.

handle.description: Canonical social handle, e.g. GitHub username. No '@' prefix.

id.description: Stable id for this entrypoint, unique within the manifest.

ref.description: Declarative reference — e.g. a relative file path, HTTP route, container image tag. Never executable code.
